result:
I'd dissolve the illusion that any one of us is separate—from each other, from the planet, from the consequences of our choices.  

Not in some fuzzy, poetic sense, but as a lived, neurological reality: the felt knowledge that your pain literally contours mine, that the river’s sickness enters my bloodstream, that tomorrow’s child is tasting the exhaust of today’s convenience.  

If that single delusion cracked—if every voter, CEO, parent, and teenager experienced interdependence as plainly as thirst—the rest of our crises would become engineering problems instead of morality plays. No law would need to preach compassion; it would simply be the most obvious path to self-interest properly understood.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
